@graph.name: BMW ActiveHybrid 3 Engine & Powertrain Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW ActiveHybrid 3 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N55 engine typically costs £4,500–£6,500 supply only. A complete PHEV powertrain (engine + electric motor) costs £5,000–£8,500. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on your location and workshop. The high-voltage battery is a separate component — replacement typically costs £3,000–£6,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW ActiveHybrid 3 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N55 straight-six is a proven, reliable engine — but it has known weak points: HPFP failure, chargepipe failure, and VANOS solenoid wear. The hybrid system adds complexity: battery degradation, electric motor / transmission integration issues, and high-voltage system concerns. The ActiveHybrid 3 is a rare, complex car — rewarding but with hybrid-specific risks.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW ActiveHybrid 3?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are HPFP failure (N55's most notorious weak point), chargepipe failure (plastic cracks), VANOS solenoid wear, hybrid battery degradation, and electric motor / transmission integration problems. The battery is the single biggest long-term concern — it can cost more to replace than the car is worth.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW ActiveHybrid 3 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on which component has failed. Against a well-kept £18,000 ActiveHybrid 3, a £5,500 N55 replacement is 31% — worth it. But if the battery has failed (£3,000–£6,000) or the complete powertrain needs replacement (£5,000–£8,500), the maths changes. A £7,000 battery + powertrain replacement against a £15,000 car is 47% — approaching our 70% BMW threshold for PHEVs. Get a battery health report before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW ActiveHybrid 3 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The ActiveHybrid 3 carries the engine code N55B30A (the same engine as the F30 335i). It's a 3.0-litre straight-six turbo petrol engine with an integrated electric motor, used in the F30 from 2013 to 2019.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the HPFP on the N55, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — HPFP replacement is a well-understood repair costing £600–£1,200. It's the N55's most common weak point. If you experience long cranking or hesitation, don't ignore it — but it's a straightforward fix, not an engine killer. The same principle applies to the ActiveHybrid 3 as to any other N55-equipped BMW.

rows.eraNote: One engine throughout this badge's history — no era ambiguity.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the N55 PHEV powertrain. The high-voltage battery is a separate component — replacement costs typically range from £3,000–£6,000 depending on battery condition and supplier.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an ActiveHybrid 3 powertrain typically takes 14–20 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). High-voltage system work requires specialist training and equipment.

cta.label: → Compare ActiveHybrid 3 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure — N55
problems.symptoms: Long cranking on startup, hesitation under acceleration, engine stalling, engine warning light, reduced power.
problems.recommendation: The N55's HPFP is its most notorious weak point. If you experience long cranking or hesitation, don't ignore it — a failing HPFP can lead to lean running and catalytic converter damage. The ActiveHybrid 3 uses the same HPFP as the standard N55, so the same warning applies. The replacement is well-understood and well-documented.

failureLink.label: Read the full HPFP failure guide

problems.title: Chargepipe Failure — N55
problems.symptoms: Loss of boost, whistling noise from the engine bay, engine warning light, reduced power.
problems.recommendation: The N55's plastic chargepipe is prone to cracking, especially on tuned cars. The solution is an aftermarket metal chargepipe — fit once and forget. This is a straightforward fix for a known weak point.

failureLink.label: Read the full chargepipe failure guide

problems.title: Hybrid Battery & Electric Motor Issues
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric-only range, rapid battery depletion, drivetrain error messages, inability to hold charge, unusual whining from the transmission.
problems.repairCost: £3,000–£6,000 (battery replacement) / £1,500–£4,000 (electric motor/inverter)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: The ActiveHybrid 3's hybrid system uses a 1.3 kWh lithium-ion battery and an electric motor integrated into the transmission. Battery degradation and electric motor faults are the main concerns. The battery is the most expensive single component to replace. If your electric range has dropped significantly or you experience drivetrain errors, have the hybrid system inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full hybrid battery degradation guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This ActiveHybrid 3? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with evidence of regular hybrid system checks
* Documented HPFP and chargepipe history (or aftermarket metal chargepipe fitted)
* No drivetrain warning lights or error codes
avoidIf: * Drivetrain warning light with no diagnosis — could indicate expensive hybrid system faults
* Significantly reduced electric range (battery degradation warning)
* No service history — the N55 is reliable but the hybrid system demands proper maintenance
* Unusual whining from the transmission (electric motor integration warning)

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The ActiveHybrid 3 is a rare, fascinating car — the blend of BMW's brilliant N55 straight-six with an electric motor. It's the most powerful F30 3 Series before the M3, and a genuine future classic. But it's a complex, low-volume car — battery degradation, HPFP issues, and electric motor faults are the price of entry. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained ActiveHybrid 3 is a £15,000–£25,000 future classic; a neglected one with a dying battery is a £5,000 gamble.

cta.label: → Compare ActiveHybrid 3 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW ActiveHybrid 3 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Battery Health — Ask for a battery health report; 70%+ capacity is BMW's warranty standard
* Electric Range — Test the electric-only range; significant reduction indicates battery degradation
* Cold Start — Listen for rattling or hesitation (HPFP or timing chain warning)
* Service History — including the ActiveHybrid 3 era and confirmed oil-change intervals using BMW LL-01 approved oil
* Chargepipe Check — Has the plastic chargepipe been replaced with an aftermarket metal unit?
* Drivetrain Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved hybrid system issues?
* Transmission Noise — Any whining or unusual noises? (electric motor integration warning)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
